articles.title: CG Weather Update: छत्तीसगढ़ में भारी बारिश के आसार, कई जिलों में बारिश के साथ आकाशीय बिजली की चेतावनी जारी - Bansal News
articles.search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Chhattisgarh
articles.full_text:
CG Weather Today: छत्तीसगढ़ में 23 जुलाई की सुबह भी मौसम सुहाना रहने के साथ कई इलाकों में बारिश होने की संभावना है। भारत मौसम विज्ञान विभाग (IMD) के अनुसार प्रदेश के अधिकांश जिलों में हल्की से मध्यम बारिश हो सकती है। मौसम विभाग का कहना है कि अगले कुछ दिनों तक मानसून सक्रिय रहेगा, इसलिए लोगों को बदलते मौसम के लिए तैयार रहने की जरूरत है।
कई जिलों में भारी बारिश का अलर्ट
मौसम विभाग ने 23 जुलाई के लिए एक-दो स्थानों पर भारी बारिश की चेतावनी जारी की है। इसके साथ ही गरज-चमक और आकाशीय बिजली गिरने की भी संभावना जताई गई है। मौसम खराब होने के दौरान खुले स्थानों पर जाने से बचने और सुरक्षित स्थान पर रहने की सलाह दी गई है।
पिछले 24 घंटे में कई जगह अच्छी बारिश
बीते 24 घंटे के दौरान प्रदेश के कई हिस्सों में अच्छी बारिश दर्ज की गई। बिलासपुर संभाग के अधिकांश इलाकों में वर्षा हुई, जबकि कुछ स्थानों पर भारी से बहुत भारी बारिश रिकॉर्ड की गई।
सबसे अधिक अधिकतम तापमान रायपुर में 30.8 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया, जबकि सबसे कम न्यूनतम तापमान पेंड्रा रोड में 22 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहा।
अगले पांच दिन तक सक्रिय रहेगा मानसून
मौसम विभाग के अनुसार अगले पांच दिनों तक पूरे प्रदेश में मानसून सक्रिय बना रहेगा। इस दौरान कई जिलों में लगातार बारिश होने की संभावना है।
23 से 27 जुलाई तक प्रदेश के अलग-अलग हिस्सों में हल्की से मध्यम बारिश के साथ कुछ स्थानों पर भारी बारिश हो सकती है। ऐसे में किसानों, यात्रियों और आम लोगों को मौसम का अपडेट देखकर ही अपनी योजना बनाने की सलाह दी गई है।

मौसम विभाग की लोगों से अपील
मौसम विभाग ने लोगों से खराब मौसम के दौरान सावधानी बरतने की अपील की है। गरज-चमक के समय पेड़ों के नीचे खड़े न हों, बिजली के उपकरणों का इस्तेमाल कम करें और सुरक्षित स्थान पर रहें। किसानों को भी फसलों और खुले में रखे सामान को सुरक्षित रखने की सलाह दी गई है।

articles.title: छत्तीसगढ़ में मानसून मेहरबान: 27 जुलाई तक भारी बारिश का अलर्ट, जानिए मौसम विभाग की नई चेतावनी - NPG News
articles.search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Chhattisgarh
articles.full_text:
छत्तीसगढ़ में अगले 5 दिनों तक मानसून पूरी तरह सक्रिय रहेगा। IMD ने 23 से 27 जुलाई तक प्रदेश के कई जिलों में मध्यम से भारी बारिश और आकाशीय बिजली गिरने का अलर्ट जारी किया है। जानिए अपने क्षेत्र का हाल...
CG Weather Update Today (23 July 2026): छत्तीसगढ़ में मानसून की चाल एक बार फिर तेज हो गई है। मौसम विभाग (IMD) के अनुसार, प्रदेश भर में बादलों का डेरा जमा हुआ है और आने वाले दिनों में हल्की से मध्यम बारिश का दौर जारी रहेगा। कई इलाकों में रिमझिम तो कहीं तेज बौछारों के कारण तापमान में गिरावट दर्ज की गई है, जिससे लोगों को उमस से राहत मिली है। मौसम विशेषज्ञों का कहना है कि प्रदेश भर में मानसून की यह सक्रियता फिलहाल बनी रहेगी।
कई जिलों के लिए जारी हुई चेतावनी
मौसम की गंभीरता को देखते हुए विभाग ने प्रदेश के कुछ स्थानों पर भारी वर्षा का अलर्ट जारी किया है। बारिश के साथ-साथ बिजली कड़कने और आकाशीय बिजली (गाज) गिरने की भी आशंका जताई गई है। ऐसे में प्रशासन और मौसम विभाग ने आम जनता को सचेत रहने की हिदायत दी है, खासकर खराब मौसम के दौरान खुले मैदानों, खेतों या पेड़ों के नीचे रुकने से बचने को कहा गया है।
पिछले 24 घंटे का हाल
बीते 24 घंटों की बात करें तो राज्य के अलग-अलग हिस्सों में अच्छी-खासी बारिश दर्ज की गई है। बिलासपुर संभाग और आसपास के इलाकों में तो कुछ जगहों पर मूसलाधार बारिश हुई है। तापमान के मामले में रायपुर 30.8 डिग्री सेल्सियस के साथ सबसे गर्म रहा, वहीं पेंड्रा रोड में न्यूनतम तापमान 22 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक गिर गया, जिससे वहां ठंडक का अहसास बढ़ा है।
27 जुलाई तक नहीं थमेगा बारिश का सिलसिला
आने वाले दिनों के पूर्वानुमान के मुताबिक, 23 से 27 जुलाई तक पूरे छत्तीसगढ़ में मानसूनी हवाएं हावी रहेंगी। इस 5 दिनों की अवधि में रुक-रुक कर बारिश का दौर चलता रहेगा। मौसम विभाग ने यात्रियों, किसानों और आम नागरिकों को सलाह दी है कि वे घर से निकलने से पहले मौसम की ताजा जानकारी जरूर चेक कर लें, ताकि किसी तरह की परेशानी न हो।
सावधानी बरतने की अपील
लगातार बदलते मौसम के बीच विभाग ने विशेष सावधानी बरतने को कहा है। गाज गिरने की घटनाओं से बचने के लिए आंधी-तूफान के वक्त पक्के मकानों में शरण लेने और बिजली के उपकरणों का इस्तेमाल सीमित करने की सलाह दी गई है। इसके साथ ही किसानों को अपनी कटी हुई फसलों और खलिहानों में रखे सामान को सुरक्षित स्थान पर ढककर रखने का सुझाव दिया गया है।
प्रियांशी नर्मदा ने गुरु घासीदास सेंट्रल यूनिवर्सिटी से जर्नलिज्म एंड मास कम्युनिकेशन (JMC) में ग्रेजुएशन किया है। फिलहाल वह IGNOU, दिल्ली से मास्टर्स कर रही हैं। कई मीडिया संस्थानों में इंटर्नशिप के बाद उन्होंने 2024 में 'TV 24' न्यूज़ चैनल से बतौर कॉपी एडिटर अपने करियर की शुरुआत की। वर्तमान में प्रियांशी NPG News से बतौर कंटेंट राइटर जुडी हैं।

articles.title: France records over 5,700 excess deaths during historic June heatwave, highest toll since 2003 - livemint.com
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Chhattisgarh
articles.full_text:
France recorded nearly 5,800 excess deaths during the June heatwave, country's deadliest heat-related episode since 2003, according to the French public health agency.
Public Health France said on Wednesday that 5,764 excess deaths were recorded between June 17 and July 2, a 36 per cent increase over the number of deaths normally expected during the period, AP reported.
The agency said the affected regions registered 21,674 deaths from all causes, compared with an expected 15,910 deaths based on statistical modelling using previous years' data.
The excess mortality is the highest recorded during any French heatwave since the devastating 2003 heatwave, which claimed an estimated 15,000 lives.
Heatwave shatters temperature records
France experienced its hottest days on record on June 24 and 25, when the national average temperature reached 30°C, accompanied by record overnight temperatures. Many parts of the country saw daytime temperatures climb above 40°C.
According to the health agency, more than half of the excess deaths occurred over just three days, from June 25 to June 27, during the peak of the heatwave.
The agency described the event as an "unprecedented episode", due to early arrival, intensity and duration, which exposed nearly the entire population while schools and workplaces remained open.
Paris region hardest hit
The Île-de-France region, which includes Paris, recorded the highest number of excess deaths.
Public Health France estimated 1,999 unexpected deaths in the Paris region, around 80 per cent higher than would normally have been expected without the heatwave.
According to AFP, funeral directors in Paris struggled to find space to store bodies before burial or cremation, with several mortuaries reaching full capacity.
Nearly half of those who died during the period passed away in hospitals, which experienced a surge in patients suffering from heat-related illnesses. Around one-third died at home, while most of the remaining deaths occurred in care homes for older people.
The health agency said the latest figures are provisional and will be updated later this year as more complete mortality data becomes available.
Elderly account for most deaths
According to The Guardian, people aged 75 years and above accounted for roughly two-thirds of the excess deaths recorded during the heatwave.
Many deaths linked to extreme heat are not officially classified as heat-related because underlying conditions such as heart attacks are often listed as the immediate cause of death.
Europe battles heat and wildfires
France's updated death toll comes as large parts of southern Europe continue to grapple with extreme temperatures and devastating wildfires.
In Spain, firefighters are battling one of the country's largest wildfires on record in the Guadalajara region, where about 32,000 hectares have burned, forcing more than 1,200 people to evacuate from 34 villages.
Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said the country has already experienced 22 major wildfires this year, four times as many as during the same period last year, and warned against downplaying the climate emergency.
In France, two firefighters died while responding to a blaze in the south-western Gironde region, while another wildfire has burned through 2,500 hectares in the Var department, forcing hundreds of residents to flee.
Authorities in Greece have imposed restrictions on outdoor work during peak daytime heat, while Italy has placed the entire island of Sicily under its highest wildfire alert as temperatures exceed 40°C and multiple fires continue to burn.

articles.title: Mumbai schools closed amid IMD’s orange alert? Institutions shut in Palghar, BMC updates on closures - The Indian Express
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Chhattisgarh
articles.full_text:
2 min readUpdated: Jul 23, 2026 02:23 PM IST
Mumbai schools closed: Schools, colleges and other educational institutions in Mumbai will remain open on Thursday even as the India Meteorological Department (IMD) upgraded its rain alert in the city to orange, predicting very heavy rainfall. The Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) has confirmed that there is no closure notice to schools and colleges amid heavy rain in Mumbai.
In Palghar, which is predicted to get extremely heavy rain amid an IMD red alert, all schools, colleges and anganwadis have been instructed to remain closed on Thursday. In Thane, which is currently seeing heavy rains, all educational establishments will remain open for students. Meanwhile, the IMD has raised its weather warning for the city to red.
Senior officials from the BMC and the Thane Municipal Corporation (TMC) confirmed that no holiday has been declared in the region, with no notification of school closures received from higher authorities until Thursday afternoon.
In the Konkan region, including Mumbai and Thane districts, a holiday is typically declared when a red alert is sounded by the IMD. A red warning is issued in the likelihood of over 204 mm rain in a span of 24 hours. While Mumbai was on yellow alert yesterday, July 22, it was raised to orange as active weather conditions being heavy downpour and strong winds.
Schools closed in Palghar amid red alert
All schools, colleges and anganwadis in Maharashtra’s Palghar district were instructed to remain closed on Thursday after the IMD issued a red alert, indicating heavy to very heavy rainfall forecast.
District Collector Dr. Indu Rani Jakhad announced the holiday as a precautionary measure, citing the possibility of waterlogging and transport disruptions. Principals, teachers and non-teaching staff have been directed to remain available to assist the administration in case of emergencies.
Meanwhile, heavy rains and severe weather will continue in Mumbai after IMD sounded an orange alert in the city. Orange alert indicates very heavy rainfall coupled with gusty winds reaching up to 60-70 kmph. According to the IMD alert for Mumbai, the heavy rains with gusty winds will continue to batter the financial capital and its neighbouring Thane and Palghar till 8.30 am on Friday, July 24.

articles.title: Mumbai schools closed: Are schools shut on Thursday, July 23, due to heavy rains? BMC shares update - The Indian Express
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Chhattisgarh
articles.full_text:
2 min readUpdated: Jul 22, 2026 01:36 PM IST
Mumbai schools closed: Schools, colleges and other educational institutions across Mumbai will remain open on Thursday, July 23, even as the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has sounded a yellow alert for the financial capital and an orange alert for the neighbouring areas of Thane, Raigad, Palghar and Ratnagiri.
Sharing an update on whether schools are closed in Mumbai or not due to heavy rains, the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) maintained that all institutions across Mumbai will remain functional today, Wednesday, and even tomorrow, Thursday.
When does BMS close schools, colleges in Mumbai?
In Mumbai, the BMC typically declares a public holiday across educational institutions when the IMD declares a ‘red’ warning for the city. owing to the possibility of intense spells of rainfall for a prolonged period.
An IMD red alert, for perspective, is sounded when the total rainfall in a span of 24 hours exceeds 204 mm rain.
However, the city is unlikely to witness such heavy spells this week with the India Meteorological Department issuing a yellow alert on Wednesday and Thursday.
While the weather bureau’s yellow alert calls for alertness amongst administration and citizens, school closure is unlikely unless the IMD upgrades its forecast warning to red warning or an orange alert when there is possibility of heavy downpour with gusty winds.
IMD yellow alert in Mumbai, orange in Thane
According to the IMD forecast, Mumbai will remain under a yellow alert until Friday, July 24, as it has been for the third day today, seeing heavy downpour.
Story continues below this ad
Thane, Palghar, Raigad and Ratnagiri have been expected to experience severe weather conditions, with IMD forecast predicting a very heavy rainfall in the regions. The IMD has sounded an orange alert in the region for the next 24 hours.
The IMD Santacruz observatory recorded 48.4 mm of rainfall, while Colaba recorded 35.6 mm. The BMC’s automatic weather system showed the western suburbs at 64.91 mm, eastern suburbs at 48.7 mm, and the island city at 36.65 mm.

articles.title: Murcia on red alert for 44ºC temperatures: Weather forecast July 23-26 - Murcia Today - News
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Chhattisgarh
articles.full_text:
- EDITIONS: Spanish News Today Alicante Today Andalucia Today
article_detail
Date Published: 23/07/2026
Murcia on red alert for 44ºC temperatures: Weather forecast July 23-26
Weather alerts on Thursday July 23
After days of relentless heat, the Region of Murcia is heading into what could be the hottest day of the summer so far. The State Meteorological Agency (Aemet) has issued its highest-level weather warning for Thursday, with temperatures forecast to reach a staggering 44ºC in some areas.
The extreme heat comes as much of the Region is already struggling through sleepless tropical nights, while forecasters are also warning of thunderstorms, strong winds and even hail later in the day. The good news is that some relief is finally on the horizon, with temperatures gradually easing from Friday onwards.
Thursday July 23
Thursday will definitely be the standout day of the week, with red weather alerts in force across the Vega del Segura, the Guadalentín Valley, Lorca and Águilas between 1pm and 9pm.
Not to labour the point, but a red weather alert signifies extreme, potentially life-threatening conditions and should always be taken very seriously.
Temperatures are expected to climb to 44ºC in the worst-affected areas this Thursday, creating conditions that health authorities warn pose a serious risk to life, particularly for older people, young children and those with existing health conditions.
Murcia city, Lorca and Yecla are all forecast to reach around 43ºC, but the heat won't be far behind elsewhere.
Orange alerts have been issued for the Altiplano, where temperatures could reach 42ºC, and the Northwest, where highs of around 40ºC are expected. Even the cooler coastal areas aren't escaping the heat, with yellow alerts covering Campo de Cartagena and Mazarrón as temperatures rise to around 38ºC.
To make matters worse, the entire Region of Murcia is also under a yellow warning for storms between 4pm and 9pm. Forecasters say the combination of intense heat and dust suspended in the atmosphere could trigger thunderstorms capable of producing hail and strong gusty winds.
Overnight temperatures will remain stubbornly high, offering little relief after an exhausting day.
Friday July 24
Friday will still be exceptionally hot, but temperatures should begin to edge down slightly inland.
The Guadalentín Valley, Lorca and Águilas remain under orange alerts, while yellow warnings continue for the Vega del Segura, Campo de Cartagena, Mazarrón and parts of the Northwest.
Skies will be partly cloudy with some high cloud and lingering Saharan dust. Light winds will dominate, although stronger southerly breezes could develop during the afternoon.
Saturday July 25
By Saturday, the heatwave should finally begin to loosen its grip on the Region.
Temperatures are expected to fall across most areas and, importantly, all heat alerts have been lifted. Skies will remain partly cloudy with some dust still hanging in the atmosphere, but conditions should feel noticeably more comfortable than earlier in the week.
Light westerly winds and coastal sea breezes will help take the edge off the heat.
Sunday July 26
Sunday brings further signs that the worst is over.
Forecasters are expecting mostly clear skies and another drop in temperatures, making it the most pleasant day of the weekend. Light winds and coastal breezes will continue and there are currently no weather warnings in force anywhere in the Region of Murcia.
Although cooler conditions are on the way, Thursday's extreme temperatures should not be taken lightly. Health officials recommend avoiding strenuous activity during the hottest part of the day, staying indoors where possible, drinking plenty of water and checking regularly on elderly relatives, neighbours and anyone who may be vulnerable.
Pets should also be kept cool and well hydrated, and drivers are advised never to leave children or animals inside parked vehicles, even for a few minutes.
Minimum and maximum temperatures in Murcia this weekend
Images: Aemet
